topSuggestions.title: 라이프 에이전트화를 scope 단위에서 domain 단위로 승격
topSuggestions.summary: 현재 domainReplacementPercent=10%, scopeReplacementPercent=37.5%입니다. 실제 업무 대체 목표에는 domain-level evidence가 필요합니다.

topSuggestions.title: LLM-Wiki 검색/재사용 ROI evidence 늘리기
topSuggestions.summary: LLM-Wiki readiness=84%이고 global cap이 남아 있습니다. 지식관리 체계가 “보이는 지도”에서 “실제로 재사용되는 시스템”으로 가려면 검색/인용/재사용 event가 더 필요합니다.

topSuggestions.title: Needs Human 결정 묶음을 더 줄이고 자동 위임 후보를 분리
topSuggestions.summary: 현재 needsHuman=3, decisionQueue=5, decisionBatches=3입니다. 결정 항목이 흩어지면 사용자가 계속 확인 병목이 됩니다.

topSuggestions.title: 외부 기술/경제 신호를 KST 일일 이슈 브리프로 승격
topSuggestions.summary: external prioritySignals=8, high=2입니다. 현재는 rolling signal이고, 사용자가 기대하는 것은 일일 판단 구간입니다.

definitions.title: Convert collected signals into ticket-ready technical definitions
definitions.goal: Turn collected external/GitHub/runtime signals into reviewable idea candidates and technical definition drafts before implementation work starts.
definitions.whyNow: The agent team can collect and triage signals, but the idea -> technical definition -> ticket-ready spec step was not a first-class agent.

sourceSignals.title: 라이프 에이전트화를 scope 단위에서 domain 단위로 승격
Sea's View on the Future of Agentic Software Development with Codex

technicalDefinition: Add a deterministic CLI agent that reads public-safe runtime artifacts.
Produce idea candidates with goal, why-now, input/output contracts, acceptance criteria, safety gates, and candidate GitHub issue payloads.
Default behavior must write local artifacts only; issue creation requires a later explicit gate.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three technical definition drafts are generated from current public-safe artifacts.
Each draft has candidate issue labels, body, acceptance criteria, verification commands, and safety/non-goals.
Check mode verifies existing artifacts without mutating GitHub or external services.
safetyAndNonGoals: No raw private data, secret values, comments scraping, external posting, deploy, payment, or destructive action.
Candidate issue payloads are drafts only and are not automatically submitted.

candidateIssue.title: Convert collected signals into ticket-ready technical definitions
candidateIssue.body:
## Goal
Turn collected external/GitHub/runtime signals into reviewable idea candidates and technical definition drafts before implementation work starts.

## Why now
The agent team can collect and triage signals, but the idea -> technical definition -> ticket-ready spec step was not a first-class agent.

## Technical definition
- Add a deterministic CLI agent that reads public-safe runtime artifacts.
- Produce idea candidates with goal, why-now, input/output contracts, acceptance criteria, safety gates, and candidate GitHub issue payloads.
- Default behavior must write local artifacts only; issue creation requires a later explicit gate.

## Inputs
- runtime/external-info-input-snapshot.json
- runtime/github-info-input-snapshot.json
- runtime/improvement-suggestions.json
- runtime/knowledge-retrieval-index.json
- runtime/content-replacement-scope-verification.json

## Outputs
- runtime/idea-technical-definitions.json
- runtime/idea-technical-definitions.md
- runtime/idea-technical-definitions-verification.json

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] At least three technical definition drafts are generated from current public-safe artifacts.
- [ ] Each draft has candidate issue labels, body, acceptance criteria, verification commands, and safety/non-goals.
- [ ] Check mode verifies existing artifacts without mutating GitHub or external services.

## Safety / non-goals
- No raw private data, secret values, comments scraping, external posting, deploy, payment, or destructive action.
- Candidate issue payloads are drafts only and are not automatically submitted.

## Verification
- `npm run idea:define`
- `npm run idea:define:check`
- `npm run agent:manifest:check`

definitions.title: Convert external tech/economy signals into daily technical brief candidates
definitions.goal: Use the external public signal route to create daily technical opportunity briefs that can be routed into implementation or watchlist tickets.
definitions.whyNow: A current external signal was collected: OpenAI and Malta partner to bring ChatGPT Plus to all citizens.

sourceSignals.title: OpenAI and Malta partner to bring ChatGPT Plus to all citizens

technicalDefinition: Group external public signals by project relevance: agentization, infrastructure, privacy, cost, knowledge, and content.
Generate one daily brief section per group with why-it-matters, affected Agent Map component, and suggested safe next action.
Route only repeated/high-score signals to candidate issue drafts; single low-confidence signals stay as watchlist items.
acceptanceCriteria: Daily brief candidates include source title, source name, link, importance, project relevance, and safe next action.
High-score repeated signals can produce candidate issue drafts; watch-only signals do not.
No full article body or comment scraping is required.
safetyAndNonGoals: No financial/legal decision is executed from a public signal.

candidateIssue.title: Convert external tech/economy signals into daily technical brief candidates
candidateIssue.body:
## Goal
Use the external public signal route to create daily technical opportunity briefs that can be routed into implementation or watchlist tickets.

## Why now
A current external signal was collected: OpenAI and Malta partner to bring ChatGPT Plus to all citizens.

## Technical definition
- Group external public signals by project relevance: agentization, infrastructure, privacy, cost, knowledge, and content.
- Generate one daily brief section per group with why-it-matters, affected Agent Map component, and suggested safe next action.
- Route only repeated/high-score signals to candidate issue drafts; single low-confidence signals stay as watchlist items.

## Inputs
- runtime/external-info-input-snapshot.json
- runtime/external-information-triage-report.md
- runtime/external-information-bounded-actions.json

## Outputs
- runtime/external-daily-technical-brief.json
- runtime/external-daily-technical-brief.md

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Daily brief candidates include source title, source name, link, importance, project relevance, and safe next action.
- [ ] High-score repeated signals can produce candidate issue drafts; watch-only signals do not.
- [ ] No full article body or comment scraping is required.

## Safety / non-goals
- Uses public RSS/Atom metadata only.
- No financial/legal decision is executed from a public signal.

## Verification
- `npm run life:loop:external-triage:check`
- `npm run idea:define:check`

definitions.title: Add privacy-boundary technical guard for life-agent serviceization
definitions.goal: Convert privacy-sensitive public signals and existing safety policy into a technical guard that prevents private raw data exposure while agentization expands.
definitions.whyNow: Recent external signals include privacy/data-sharing risk, while the project is moving toward serviceized life-agent workflows.

sourceSignals.title: Databricks brings GPT-5.5 to enterprise agent workflows

technicalDefinition: Define a reusable public-safe artifact contract with fields allowed, redacted, and forbidden.
Add a verification step that scans candidate public artifacts for secret/private/raw-data markers before build/deploy.
Integrate the guard with life-agent replacement scopes and service-integration evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Every replacement scope declares allowed, redacted, and forbidden fields.
Public runtime artifacts are checked before static artifact publication.
Failures block policy/build checks without exposing secret values.
safetyAndNonGoals: Does not read raw Kakao/private source data.
Does not relax any existing publish/upload/deploy/payment gates.

candidateIssue.title: Add privacy-boundary technical guard for life-agent serviceization
candidateIssue.body:
## Goal
Convert privacy-sensitive public signals and existing safety policy into a technical guard that prevents private raw data exposure while agentization expands.

## Why now
Recent external signals include privacy/data-sharing risk, while the project is moving toward serviceized life-agent workflows.

## Technical definition
- Define a reusable public-safe artifact contract with fields allowed, redacted, and forbidden.
- Add a verification step that scans candidate public artifacts for secret/private/raw-data markers before build/deploy.
- Integrate the guard with life-agent replacement scopes and service-integration evidence.

## Inputs
- config/life-agentization-scorecard.json
- config/service-integration.json
- runtime/content-replacement-scope-verification.json

## Outputs
- runtime/privacy-boundary-technical-guard.json
- docs/PRIVACY_BOUNDARY_TECHNICAL_GUARD.md

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every replacement scope declares allowed, redacted, and forbidden fields.
- [ ] Public runtime artifacts are checked before static artifact publication.
- [ ] Failures block policy/build checks without exposing secret values.

## Safety / non-goals
- Does not read raw Kakao/private source data.
- Does not relax any existing publish/upload/deploy/payment gates.

## Verification
- `npm run policy:check`
- `npm run artifact:check`
